_comment: Crowdsourced hardware-capability database. One entry per firmware revision of a pad, keyed by VID:PID. Submit yours via the Hardware Report issue template (paste `freemicro detect --json`). Fields marked null are UNVERIFIED. The full wire protocol lives in docs/PROTOCOL.md.

devices.name: OpenAI Codex Micro (Work Louder)
devices.macosAccessMethod: IOKit IOHIDDeviceOpen via IOServiceGetMatchingServices('IOHIDDevice'); requires Input Monitoring. hidapi open_path() ALWAYS FAILS on macOS because hidapi models each HID top-level collection as a separate openable path while macOS vends ONE IOHIDDevice whose primary usage is the keyboard collection and which contains 0xFF00 as a secondary collection.

protocol.framing: USB: [0x02][len][utf-8 JSON] in 63 bytes. BLE: [0x06][0x02][len][utf-8 JSON] in 64 bytes. Messages CRLF-terminated; long messages span multiple reports.
protocol.encoding: JSON-RPC; compact {m,p,id} and standard {jsonrpc,method,params,id} both accepted
protocol.writeReturnCodesAreMeaningless: A wrongly framed write still returns kIOReturnSuccess and is silently discarded. The ONLY reliable health check is a device.status round trip.

keys.params: {k, act, ag} - k=key id, act 1=down/0=up, ag=agent index
keys.note: The keys emit NO ordinary keyboard scancodes. Nothing types unless software listens on this channel.
keys.encoderRotation: ENC_CW / ENC_CC arrive through v.oai.hid like the keys, one event per detent. Their 'act' value is NOT reliably 1 - fire ticks on any act value; they are momentary and have no matching release, so nothing can double-trigger.
keys.physicalLayout: ACT06=LAB, ACT07=PR, ACT08=NAV, ACT09=PLAY, ACT10=TERM, ACT11=MIC, ACT12=spare (one unit, confirmed)

joystick.note: Analogue thumbstick, definitively - it sweeps angle and returns to exactly {a:0,d:0} on release, which an encoder cannot do.

lighting.liveFields: minimized names: e=effect, b=brightness 0-1, s=speed 0-1, m=magic (UNVERIFIED), c=packed 0xRRGGBB int
lighting.agentKeyParams: ARRAY of {id, c, b, e, s, sk?, sa?} - id 0-5, one entry per Agent Key; verified visually setting all six independently
lighting.storedConfigNote: RESOLVED 2026-07-23: v.oai.rgbcfg is the LIVE path and visibly drives the underglow and key backlight. The earlier 'rgbcfg does nothing' finding was a false negative from malformed BLE writes made before the 64-byte report-id-prefixed framing was discovered. The vendor app uses rgbcfg + thstatus and never calls lights.preview.
lighting.notificationsTakeNoId: Every v.oai.* method is a notification - sending one WITH an id returns 404.
lighting.replacesPreviousState: Each lighting call replaces the previous one, so a fast sequence looks like only its final frame to a human observer. Hold each colour 1-2s when testing by eye.
lighting.previewMethodNote: lights.preview EXISTS in the firmware method table and returns {'result': null}, but produces NO visible change on firmware v0.4.1 - neither zone, over USB or Bluetooth, with the vendor app quit. Verified 2026-07-23. Do not use it.

baseMethods: sys.bootloader (WARNING: reboots into DFU and disconnects the device - never call during normal operation)

hidInterfaces.note: Vendor-defined - the JSON-RPC channel carrying key events AND lighting

devices.notes: VERIFIED on a shipping unit, 2026-07-23, macOS. (1) Enumerates as VID 0x303A (Espressif) / PID 0x8360, mfr 'Work Louder', product 'Codex Micro'. The MCU is ESP32-family, NOT the RP2040 assumed for the Creator Micro 2 - so Path C (reflash the open-source RP2040-only work_louder/micro QMK target) is INVALID for this silicon. (2) No 0xFF60 VIA/raw-HID channel exists -> Path A (VIA) is BLOCKED. (3) The 0xFF00 vendor channel speaks JSON-RPC, not an opaque binary protocol - INPUTS and LEDS are both solved through it, with no USB capture required. Report descriptor captured (275 bytes, hardware/probes/codex-report-descriptor.bin): usage page 0xFF00, usage 1, Report ID 6, 63-byte Input AND Output reports. (4) An earlier write test concluded 'writes are accepted but every payload is ignored' - accurate but misleading: the framing was right, the payload simply was not JSON the firmware recognised. (5) The unit exhibited severe USB intermittency (enumerating for seconds then dropping), unrelated to writes - it began before any write was issued. (6) Everything here is one unit on one firmware revision; a firmware update could change any of it.
openQuestions: Does v.oai.rgbcfg actually drive the lights? (see lighting.storedConfigNote)
The literal 'act' value on encoder rotation events
Linux / Windows host access to the 0xFF00 channel (should be reachable; unimplemented)
Whether a synthetic fn modifier triggers third-party dictation apps
Whether the bootloader accepts third-party firmware
reportedBy: local probe (macOS, hidapi + IOKit, USB and Bluetooth)

bluetoothLE.writeFraming: [0x06][0x02][len][json], 64 bytes, reportID=6, Output - the report id is REPEATED as buffer[0]
bluetoothLE.note: Fully capable: input, lighting and RPC all verified wirelessly with the cable unplugged. Match on VID/PID only - the product string and collection count differ from USB.

transports.note: Detect via the IOHIDDevice 'Transport' registry property ('USB' vs 'Bluetooth Low Energy') and select the write framing from it. Nothing else is conditioned on transport.

factoryDefaults.notes: Green means UNREAD, not completed - it clears when the user looks at the thread. The key backlight is dark almost always. Auto-dim after 180s means FULLY dark, not reduced brightness. Battery and transport have no effect on lighting.

safety: NEVER call sys.bootloader in normal operation - it reboots into DFU and disconnects the device.
fs.write / fs.delete have no documented restore path on this silicon; out of scope.

The IOKit input-report callback plus a CFRunLoop segfaults if the ctypes callback is collected while the pad drops mid-call. Hold a strong reference, never raise inside the callback, and prefer callback-free fire-and-forget writes for send-only paths.
